# CWE-1286 negative: strict JSON syntax and URL validation (pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-1286-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

// CWE-1286 mitigated: decoder rejects unknown fields and validates URL syntax.

import (
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/json"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
)

type HookConfigValidatedPure struct {
	URL     string `json:"url"`
	Enabled bool   `json:"enabled"`
}

func SaveHookConfigValidatedPure(db *sql.DB) http.HandlerFunc {
	return func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		dec := json.NewDecoder(r.Body)
		dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
		var cfg HookConfigValidatedPure
		if err := dec.Decode(&cfg); err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		parsed, err := url.ParseRequestURI(cfg.URL)
		if err != nil || parsed.Scheme != "https" {
			http.Error(w, "invalid hook url", http.StatusBadRequest)
			return
		}
		_, err = db.Exec(
			"INSERT INTO hook_configs (url, enabled) VALUES ($1, $2)", cfg.URL, cfg.Enabled,
		)
		if err != nil {
			http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
			return
		}
		w.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
		w.Write([]byte(`{"saved":true}`))
	}
}
